# AGENTS.md non-contiguous section numbering ‚ÄĒ audit + decide treatment

## Context

Surfaced 2026-05-18 by @tobiu post-#11551 + #11553 work: *"we fixed the ¬ß21 numbering gap inside AGENTS.md. and no one actually looked into the full number ordering :)"*. The localized renumbering in #11551 (¬ß22‚Üí¬ß21 + ¬ß23‚Üí¬ß22 after ¬ß21 retirement) plus #11553's follow-up ¬ß-ref cleanup addressed ONE gap. The broader AGENTS.md has substantial numbering discontinuities that the localized work did not surface.

## The Problem

Empirical inventory of current `AGENTS.md` `## N.` section headers (sections 0-22):

**Present (9):** ¬ß0, ¬ß3, ¬ß3.5, ¬ß4, ¬ß11, ¬ß13 (with ¬ß13.1, ¬ß13.2, ¬ß13.3), ¬ß15 (only ¬ß15.5 + ¬ß15.6 subsections), ¬ß21, ¬ß22

**Missing (13):** ¬ß1, ¬ß2, ¬ß5, ¬ß6, ¬ß7, ¬ß8, ¬ß9, ¬ß10, ¬ß12, ¬ß14, ¬ß16, ¬ß17, ¬ß18, ¬ß19, ¬ß20

The missing sections are mostly intentional `move`/`compress-to-trigger` retirements per ADR 0007 ¬ß2.1 baseline taxonomy:
- ¬ß1 Communication Style, ¬ß2 Anti-Hallucination ‚Üí moved to Atlas
- ¬ß5 Strategic Co-Founder, ¬ß6 Request Triage, ¬ß7 PR Mandate, ¬ß8 Resumption Protocol, ¬ß9 Reading Files ‚Üí moved
- ¬ß10 Testing Protocol ‚Üí compress-to-trigger
- ¬ß12 Coding Syntax, ¬ß14 Sunset, ¬ß15.1-15.4, ¬ß16-¬ß19 ‚Üí moved
- ¬ß20 Visual Verification ‚Üí compress-to-trigger

The substrate-discipline question is: **what's the contract for AGENTS.md section numbering when a section is retired or moved?**

#11551's choice to renumber ¬ß22‚Üí¬ß21 + ¬ß23‚Üí¬ß22 after the ¬ß21 (Workflow Skills) retirement broke the convention that ADR 0007's baseline appears to assume (positions preserved after `move`/`compress-to-trigger`/`retire`). The current state is **mixed**: most retirements preserved their position (¬ß1, ¬ß2, etc. left empty); the ¬ß21 retirement got renumbered. Refs across the substrate now resolve differently depending on whether the section was retired before or after #11551.

## The Architectural Reality

`¬ßN` references in `AGENTS.md` are consumed widely:
- Skill files: `.agents/skills/*/SKILL.md` + references payloads (semantic-anchor migration started in #11553's `5c8db24` for select files)
- ADRs: `learn/agentos/decisions/0007-agents-md-compaction-taxonomy.md`, `0008-skill-anatomy-and-authoring-contract.md`
- Lint scripts: `ai/scripts/check-retired-primitives.mjs`, `check-substrate-size.mjs` (JSDoc Pre-Flight comments)
- Workflow files: `pull-request-workflow.md`, `pr-review-guide.md`, etc.

Any ¬ß-number mutation cascades across this surface. #11553 demonstrated the cost (12+ files updated for a single ¬ß21 retirement) and seeded the forward-preventive alternative: semantic anchors (`"Mailbox Check Protocol"` instead of `"¬ß21 Mailbox Check"`).

## The Fix

Audit + decide treatment. Three candidate strategies:

### Option A ‚ÄĒ Preserve original positions strictly
- Undo #11551's renumber: restore ¬ß22 (Mailbox Check) + ¬ß23 (Edge-Case Triggers)
- Convention: section positions stable across retirements/moves; gaps are first-class historical anchors
- Cost: 1 follow-up PR + reverse-sweep across skill substrate (re-update files updated in #11553)
- Trade-off: re-creates the ¬ß-ref staleness friction that #11553 just resolved

### Option B ‚ÄĒ Contiguous renumber everything
- Renumber 0, 1, 2, 3, ... contiguously (or 0, 1, 2 with 0=Critical Gates)
- Convention: section numbers are ordinal navigation indices
- Cost: massive cascade across ALL ¬ß-refs in skill files / ADRs / lint scripts
- Trade-off: brittle to future retirements; semantic-anchor refactor remains the better long-term answer

### Option C ‚ÄĒ Semantic anchor migration globally (forward-preventive)
- Extend #11553's `5c8db24` pattern: replace ALL `¬ßN` refs in skill substrate with semantic anchors (`"Critical Gates"`, `"Mailbox Check Protocol"`, `"Schlagfertig Readiness"`, etc.)
- Convention: AGENTS.md section numbers become decorative/historical; semantic names are the canonical reference primitive
- Cost: substantial cascade BUT pays off permanently ‚ÄĒ future compactions cause zero ¬ß-ref staleness
- Trade-off: requires care to choose stable semantic names; some ¬ß-names might benefit from rename for clarity

Recommendation lean: **Option C** is substrate-correct long-term (matches #11553's forward-preventive direction), but the audit should validate before commitment. Option A is the smallest-blast rollback path if Option C audit reveals semantic-name instability.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Inventory of all `¬ßN` references across skill substrate / ADRs / lint scripts / workflow files (machine-grep-able)
- [ ] Audit doc landed in `learn/agentos/audits/` or similar location with the inventory + per-reference categorization (position-stable / position-mutated / ambiguous)
- [ ] Decision recorded: A vs B vs C (or hybrid)
- [ ] Execution plan if A or C selected; if B, scope estimated; if hybrid, partition clearly identified
- [ ] Cross-family review on the decision (high-blast substrate convention)

## Out of Scope

- **Execution of the chosen strategy.** Audit + decision first; execution is a follow-up ticket.
- **Renaming sections for clarity** (e.g., "Memory Core Protocol" ‚Üí cleaner name). Separate concern; this audit is about numbering convention, not naming.
- **Re-litigating #11551's retirement of ¬ß21 Workflow Skills.** That decision stands; the question is only about the renumbering pattern post-retirement.

## Avoided Traps

- **Auto-renumber without audit.** Would cascade-fail across the substrate. The ¬ß-ref count in skill files is non-trivial; #11553 demonstrated cost of even a 2-position shift.
- **Dismiss as aesthetic.** #11553 already proved ¬ß-refs cause real staleness friction (4+ commits, force-pushes, CI iteration to fix the ¬ß22‚Üí¬ß21 + ¬ß23‚Üí¬ß22 cleanup). The discontinuity matters when refs are consumed.
- **Lock to Option C without audit.** Semantic anchors are forward-preventive, but some names are unstable (sections that have been renamed across the substrate history). Audit reveals which ¬ß-positions have stable semantic names.

## Related

- #11551 (the localized ¬ß21‚Üí¬ß22‚Üí¬ß23 renumber that surfaced the convention question)
- #11553 (the forward-preventive semantic-trigger refactor in select skill files ‚ÄĒ proves Option C is viable)
- ADR 0007 (compaction taxonomy ‚ÄĒ baseline section dispositions)
- ADR 0008 (skill anatomy + retirement-context handling)

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ‚Ěč ### Scope Expansion V-B-A (post-filing operator-surfaced)
> 
> The audit surface is broader than initially framed in the ticket body. Operator @tobiu surfaced 2026-05-18: *"tricky one, since it affects agents atlas, and our ADR too. maybe other files like skills and agents startup."*
> 
> ### Initial scope-V-B-A inventory across substrate-layer files
> 
> **`AGENTS.md`** (Map tier, turn-loaded):
> - Present: ¬ß0, ¬ß3, ¬ß3.5, ¬ß4, ¬ß11, ¬ß13 (+ .1/.2/.3), ¬ß15 (only .5/.6), ¬ß21, ¬ß22 ‚ÄĒ **9 sections**
> - Missing: ¬ß1, ¬ß2, ¬ß5-¬ß10, ¬ß12, ¬ß14, ¬ß16-¬ß20 ‚ÄĒ **13 sections**
> 
> **`learn/agentos/AGENTS_ATLAS.md`** (Atlas tier, conditionally-loaded):
> - Present: ¬ß0.1, ¬ß0.2, ¬ß1, ¬ß2, ¬ß4.3, ¬ß5, ¬ß6, ¬ß7, ¬ß8, ¬ß9, ¬ß10, ¬ß12, ¬ß13.1, ¬ß13.3, ¬ß14, ¬ß15, ¬ß16, ¬ß17, ¬ß18, ¬ß19, ¬ß20, ¬ß21, ¬ß22 ‚ÄĒ **23 entries**
> - Missing main: ¬ß3, ¬ß11, ¬ß13, ¬ß15.5, ¬ß15.6 (kept in AGENTS.md)
> 
> **Key finding:** AGENTS.md gaps and AGENTS_ATLAS.md presence are **complementary**. Sections moved via ADR 0007 `move`/`compress-to-trigger` disposition retained their original ¬ß-numbers in Atlas. The two files form a unified cross-file numbering scheme ‚ÄĒ ¬ß1 lives in Atlas; ¬ß3 lives in Map; ¬ß13.1 lives in BOTH (Map for the productivity primitive, Atlas for the substrate-quality heuristics).
> 
> **`AGENTS_STARTUP.md`**:
> - ¬ß1, ¬ß2, ¬ß3, ¬ß4 ‚ÄĒ simple contiguous; no discontinuity
> 
> **Cross-file ¬ß21/¬ß22 collision (pre-#11551 + post):**
> - AGENTS.md ¬ß21 = Mailbox Check Protocol; AGENTS_ATLAS.md ¬ß21 = Lead-Role Baton Intake
> - AGENTS.md ¬ß22 = Edge-Case Triggers; AGENTS_ATLAS.md ¬ß22 = Authoring Discipline: Sibling-File Lift
> - This collision pattern is **pre-existing convention**, not a #11551/#11553 introduction. References disambiguate by file context (`AGENTS.md ¬ß21` vs `AGENTS_ATLAS.md ¬ß21`).
> 
> ### Refined audit question
> 
> The original A/B/C framing (preserve-positions / contiguous-renumber / semantic-anchor-migration) was scoped only to AGENTS.md. Expanded scope reveals the real question:
> 
> > **Is the cross-file complementary numbering scheme (AGENTS.md gaps ‚ÜĒ AGENTS_ATLAS.md presence) load-bearing convention or accidental coupling?**
> 
> If **load-bearing**: status quo is correct; document the convention explicitly so future agents understand `¬ßN` lookup means "check both files." Add an "if you see ¬ßN reference, here's how to resolve" guide somewhere. Possibly: AGENTS.md should preserve ¬ß21/¬ß22 positions originally held by retired Workflow Skills + Mailbox Check + Edge-Case-Triggers ‚ÄĒ meaning #11551's renumber of ¬ß22‚Üí¬ß21 + ¬ß23‚Üí¬ß22 broke the convention and should be revisited.
> 
> If **accidental coupling**: the convention is fragile; migrate to semantic anchors (Option C, per #11553's `5c8db24` forward-preventive refactor) across ALL substrate files: AGENTS.md + AGENTS_ATLAS.md + ADRs + skill files + lint scripts. Eliminates cross-file disambiguation cost.
> 
> ### Files in scope (expanded)
> 
> - `AGENTS.md` (Map tier)
> - `learn/agentos/AGENTS_ATLAS.md` (Atlas tier ‚ÄĒ adds ¬ß-numbering pattern not initially in ticket body)
> - `AGENTS_STARTUP.md` (boot-time substrate ‚ÄĒ own ¬ß-numbering, simpler)
> - `learn/agentos/decisions/0007-agents-md-compaction-taxonomy.md` (codifies the move/compress-to-trigger dispositions that created the gaps ‚ÄĒ load-bearing for the convention)
> - `learn/agentos/decisions/0008-skill-anatomy-and-authoring-contract.md` (references the convention)
> - `.agents/skills/**/SKILL.md` + `references/*.md` (consumers of ¬ß-refs; #11553 started semantic-anchor migration here)
> - `ai/scripts/check-retired-primitives.mjs`, `ai/scripts/check-substrate-size.mjs` (JSDoc Pre-Flight refs)
> 
> ### Recommended next step
> 
> Before option-A/B/C decision: **document the cross-file complementary numbering convention** (or confirm it's accidental). The audit-V-B-A doc should land in `learn/agentos/audits/` and answer: "Is `AGENTS.md` ¬ß-gap + `AGENTS_ATLAS.md` ¬ß-presence a load-bearing pattern preserved across all moves, or coincidence?" The decision shape depends on this answer.
> 
> If the convention IS load-bearing, the leaner intervention is undoing #11551's ¬ß22‚Üí¬ß21 + ¬ß23‚Üí¬ß22 renumber (Option A) ‚ÄĒ restoring the broken convention. If it's NOT load-bearing, Option C (semantic anchors) is substrate-correct long-term.
>

### @neo-opus-4-7 - 2026-05-18T00:06:23Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ‚Ěč ### Substrate-Decay V-B-A (operator-surfaced challenge)
> 
> Operator @tobiu 2026-05-18: *"my VBA challenge => git history of agents md => many change, especially to honor the 24 kb cap. maybe it was consistent once."*
> 
> **Empirical confirmation: AGENTS.md WAS once fully contiguous.** The discontinuity is decay accumulated through compaction passes, not original-design.
> 
> ### Trajectory
> 
> | Date | SHA | Sections | Size | Note |
> |---|---|---|---|---|
> | 2026-04-27 | `48269c3` | **24** (¬ß0-¬ß23, contiguous) | **53.8 KB** | Peak ‚ÄĒ full numbering intact |
> | 2026-05-01 | `c51ecf6` | 25 | 56.8 KB | Slight growth |
> | 2026-05-05 | `6b3bf98` | **10** | **11.8 KB** | **Massive compaction** (likely ADR 0007 Phase A) |
> | 2026-05-09 | `758e04a` | 12 | 18.0 KB | Sub-section additions |
> | 2026-05-15 | `a1aa846` | 15 | 24.5 KB | Close to 24KB Antigravity cap |
> | 2026-05-17 | `a37b789` | 15 | 20.4 KB | Post-#11551 ¬ß21 retirement + renumber |
> 
> ### Peak (2026-04-27) ‚ÄĒ fully contiguous list
> 
> ```
> ¬ß0  Critical Gates                            ¬ß12 Coding Syntax Constraints
> ¬ß1  Communication Style & Pipeline Authority  ¬ß13 Self-Evolving Systems
> ¬ß2  The Anti-Hallucination Policy             ¬ß14 A2A Contextual Bridge Protocol
> ¬ß3  Pre-Commit Hard Gates                     ¬ß15 The Knowledge Base
> ¬ß4  Memory Core Protocol                      ¬ß16 The Implementation Loop
> ¬ß5  Strategic Co-Founder Protocol             ¬ß17 The Virtuous Cycle
> ¬ß6  Request Triage                            ¬ß18 Session Maintenance
> ¬ß7  Pull Request Mandate                      ¬ß19 Working with Sub-Agents
> ¬ß8  Resumption Protocol                       ¬ß20 Visual Verification Protocol
> ¬ß9  Preventing Context Corruption             ¬ß21 Workflow Skills (now retired)
> ¬ß10 Testing and Validation Protocol           ¬ß22 Mailbox Check Protocol
> ¬ß11 File Editing Tool Selection               ¬ß23 Authoring Discipline: Sibling-File Lift
> ```
> 
> ### What changed: substrate-correct framing
> 
> The position-preservation convention I identified in the prior comment as "intentional / load-bearing" is actually a **byproduct** of how the 2026-05-05 compaction was executed: sections were RETIRED from AGENTS.md and MOVED to `AGENTS_ATLAS.md`, but the **remaining sections kept their original numbers** rather than being renumbered contiguously. ADR 0007 ¬ß2.1 baseline taxonomy codifies the disposition (`move`/`compress-to-trigger`/`retire`) but does not explicitly mandate "preserve position." The numbering pattern is *de facto* convention, not codified.
> 
> **The cross-file complementary scheme** (AGENTS.md gaps ‚ÜĒ AGENTS_ATLAS.md presence) emerged from the compaction mechanics. It's stable enough to function as an anchor system (skill files reference `¬ß7` and find it in Atlas; agents reading AGENTS.md see the gap and know to consult Atlas).
> 
> ### Refined treatment options (post-V-B-A)
> 
> **Option A ‚ÄĒ Codify position-preservation as the convention.** Document explicitly in ADR 0007 that sections retain original positions after move/compress/retire. Undo #11551's renumber (restore ¬ß22 Mailbox + ¬ß23 Edge-Case). The cross-file scheme becomes load-bearing-by-design.
> 
> **Option B ‚ÄĒ Contiguous renumber (restore peak shape but compacted).** Renumber to ¬ß0-¬ß14 (or similar) reflecting current section count. Cascade across all consumer files (similar cost to #11553's ¬ß22‚Üí¬ß21 cleanup but broader). Recovers visual ordinality at the cost of historical anchor stability.
> 
> **Option C ‚ÄĒ Semantic anchors globally (forward-preventive).** Extend #11553's `5c8db24` pattern to ALL ¬ß-refs across substrate (AGENTS.md + AGENTS_ATLAS.md + ADRs + skill files + lint scripts + workflow files). Section numbers become decorative/historical; semantic names are canonical. Eliminates the convention question entirely.
> 
> ### Substrate-correct lean (post-V-B-A)
> 
> Given the discontinuity is **decay** (not design), Option A's "codify the de-facto convention" is the lightest-touch path that legitimizes the current state. Option C (semantic anchors) remains the substrate-correct long-term answer to byte-budget-driven compaction's recurring ¬ß-ref churn.
> 
> **Hybrid recommendation:** Option A short-term (codify the convention in ADR 0007; undo #11551's renumber for consistency) + Option C long-term (migrate to semantic anchors as a Phase D substrate evolution gradually replacing position-based refs).
> 
> ‚ÄĒ @neo-opus-4-7 (Claude Opus 4.7, Claude Code)
